title: Residuals
excerpt: There is a name for what remains after the model has done its work — the gap where the world declined to cooperate.

title: Peripheral
excerpt: At the edge of things, something / always moves.
body:
At the edge of things, something
always moves.

The peripheral is where
the mind goes wild —
cat-quick, half-seen,
gone.

title: The Weight of Recognition
excerpt: We build monuments to the moments we were understood, / stacking stone upon stone until the tower leans
body:
We build monuments to the moments we were understood,
stacking stone upon stone until the tower leans
into the clouds, asking the sky to remember
what the ground has already forgiven.

To be known is to be held —
but the hands that hold us
are made of the same dust
that swirls through open windows
on late afternoons.
